name: Lebanon

colors.symbolism: the blood shed in defence of Lebanon against successive invaders
peace; the snow of Mount Lebanon, a symbol of purity and the country's enduring nature
the colour of the cedar of Lebanon; immortality and steadfastness
the trunk of the cedar of Lebanon

description: Three horizontal stripes — red over white over red — with the central white stripe twice as tall as each red stripe. Centred on the white stripe is a green cedar of Lebanon with a brown trunk; the cedar's height equals the height of the white stripe and it touches the upper and lower red stripes.
history: Adopted on 7 December 1943 just before Lebanon's full independence from France (announced 22 November 1943). The flag was designed by members of parliament during the independence movement; the cedar — already a Lebanese symbol on the flag of the Mandate of Lebanon (1920) and on much earlier emblems — replaces the French tricolour canton of the mandate flag. The two red stripes are inspired by traditional flags of the Maronite military orders, while the proportions (1:2:1) ensure that the cedar fits entirely within the white stripe.

features.symbolism: the red stripes recall the blood shed by Lebanese in defence of the country against successive invaders; the central white stripe represents peace and the snow of Mount Lebanon

the cedar of Lebanon (Cedrus libani), a tree native to the country and mentioned 75 times in the Hebrew Bible; symbol of holiness, eternity and peace, and a representation of the Lebanese people through millennia of invasions
